title: Singapore Students Excel in International Mathematics and Science Study
excerpt: Singapore's primary and secondary students continue to top the global rankings in mathematics and science, according to the latest TIMSS results.
body:
Singapore's students have once again demonstrated outstanding performance in the Trends in International Mathematics and Science Study (TIMSS) 2025, securing top positions in both mathematics and science at the Primary 4 and Secondary 2 levels.

The results reflect the dedication of our educators, the support of parents, and the effectiveness of Singapore's mathematics and science curricula. The Ministry of Education commends all students and teachers for their hard work.

MOE will continue to review and enhance our curriculum to ensure that our students remain well-prepared for the challenges of the future economy. Initiatives such as the Applied Learning Programme and the Introduction of Artificial Intelligence modules in schools are part of ongoing efforts to strengthen STEM education.

Key Highlights

  • Singapore ranked first in both mathematics and science at P4 and S2 levels
  • Significant improvement in problem-solving skills among students
  • Gender gap in mathematics performance has narrowed considerably
alt: Students in a science laboratory conducting experiments

title: MOE Announces Enhanced Financial Support for Higher Education
excerpt: New bursaries and grants will make higher education more accessible for students from lower-income families starting Academic Year 2026.
body:
The Ministry of Education has announced a comprehensive package of enhanced financial support measures aimed at ensuring that no student is denied access to higher education due to financial constraints.

Starting from Academic Year 2026, the new measures include increased bursary amounts, expanded eligibility criteria, and new grants to cover living expenses. The enhancements are expected to benefit over 30,000 students annually.

"Every Singaporean should have the opportunity to pursue their aspirations through education, regardless of their financial background," said the Minister for Education. "These enhancements reflect our commitment to building a more inclusive and equitable education system."

Key Measures

  • Increase in MOE Bursary amounts by up to 20%
  • Expansion of income eligibility criteria to cover more families
  • New Living Expenses Grant for students from lower-income households
  • Enhanced support for part-time students
alt: Students studying at a university campus

title: Minister for Education Speaks at International Education Summit
excerpt: Minister Chan highlighted Singapore's approach to AI in education and lifelong learning at the Global Education Forum in Helsinki.
body:
The Minister for Education, Mr Chan Chun Sing, delivered a keynote address at the Global Education Forum in Helsinki, where he shared Singapore's strategic approach to integrating Artificial Intelligence into the education system.

In his speech, Minister Chan emphasised the importance of preparing students for a future where AI will be ubiquitous. He outlined Singapore's balanced approach, which focuses on leveraging AI to enhance teaching and learning while also teaching students about the ethical implications of AI.

"We must equip our students not just with the skills to use AI, but with the wisdom to use it responsibly," said Minister Chan. "Our goal is to create an education system that is both technologically advanced and deeply human-centric."

The Minister also highlighted Singapore's commitment to lifelong learning, noting that SkillsFuture will continue to be a key pillar of Singapore's education strategy.
alt: Minister speaking at a podium at an international conference

title: New School Curriculum to Include Digital Literacy from Primary 1
excerpt: Digital literacy will be introduced as a core component of the primary school curriculum from 2027, equipping students with essential tech skills.
body:
The Ministry of Education has announced that digital literacy will become a core component of the primary school curriculum starting from the 2027 academic year. Students from Primary 1 will begin learning fundamental digital skills including typing, online safety, and basic coding.

The new curriculum framework has been developed in consultation with educators, technology experts, and child development specialists. It is designed to be age-appropriate and progressively builds on skills from primary through secondary education.

"In today's digital age, basic digital literacy is as essential as reading, writing, and arithmetic," said the Permanent Secretary for Education. "This initiative ensures that our students are prepared for a digital future from the very start of their educational journey."
alt: Young children learning on tablets in a classroom

title: MOE Launches New Teacher Training Programme for Special Needs Education
excerpt: A specialised teacher training programme aims to better equip educators to support students with special educational needs in mainstream schools.
body:
The Ministry of Education has launched a new Specialist Diploma in Special Needs Education to train teachers in supporting students with diverse learning needs. The programme will be offered in partnership with the National Institute of Education.

This initiative is part of MOE's commitment to inclusive education, ensuring that all students, regardless of their learning needs, receive quality education in an environment that supports their growth.

The programme covers areas such as understanding different learning disabilities, designing inclusive lesson plans, and using assistive technology in the classroom.

title: Singapore Ranks Top in Global Digital Skills Index for Youth
excerpt: Singapore's youth have been ranked first globally in digital skills proficiency, according to the International Digital Literacy Index 2025.
body:
Singapore has achieved the top ranking in the International Digital Literacy Index (IDLI) 2025, which measures digital skills proficiency among 15-24 year olds across 50 countries. The index assesses competencies including information literacy, digital communication, content creation, and online safety.

This achievement underscores the success of Singapore's comprehensive approach to digital education, which integrates technology across the curriculum from primary school through to tertiary education.
alt: Youth working on laptops in a modern classroom

title: New SkillsFuture Credits Top-Up for Mid-Career Workers
excerpt: Mid-career workers will receive an additional SkillsFuture Credits top-up to support reskilling and upskilling in emerging industries.
body:
The Ministry of Education has announced a S$500 top-up in SkillsFuture Credits for all Singaporeans aged 40 and above. The top-up is part of the government's ongoing efforts to support mid-career workers in adapting to changes in the economy.

The enhanced credits can be used for a wide range of training programmes, including courses in artificial intelligence, green economy skills, and healthcare sectors.
alt: Adult learners in a classroom setting

title: Speech at the Appointment Ceremony for New School Principals
excerpt: Minister Chan addresses newly appointed principals, emphasising leadership and the evolving role of schools in community building.
body:
Minister for Education Mr Chan Chun Sing delivered an inspiring speech at the Appointment Ceremony for new school principals, highlighting the critical role that school leaders play in shaping the next generation.

"As principals, you are not just administrators of schools. You are the architects of learning environments where our children discover their passions, develop their character, and build their futures," said Minister Chan.
alt: Minister with newly appointed school principals

title: MOE Partners with Tech Industry to Launch AI Learning Lab in Schools
excerpt: A new AI Learning Lab will be established in 20 secondary schools to give students hands-on experience with artificial intelligence technologies.
body:
The Ministry of Education has partnered with leading technology companies to establish AI Learning Labs in 20 secondary schools across Singapore. The labs will provide students with hands-on experience in AI technologies, including machine learning, natural language processing, and computer vision.

The partnership includes curriculum development support, teacher training, and equipment sponsorship. The initiative aims to prepare students for the AI-driven economy and encourage interest in STEM careers.
alt: Students working with AI equipment in a lab setting

title: National School Games 2026 Registration Now Open
excerpt: Registration for the 2026 National School Games is now open, with new sports categories including esports and obstacle course racing.
body:
The Ministry of Education has announced that registration for the National School Games (NSG) 2026 is now open. This year's edition introduces two new sports categories: esports and obstacle course racing, reflecting evolving student interests.

The NSG remains a key platform for promoting holistic education through sports, fostering values such as teamwork, resilience, and sportsmanship among students.
alt: Students participating in a school sports event
